# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Create a Deployment Manager template for Cloud SQL PostgreSQL instance. Configure authorized networks to allow connections from anywhere (0.0.0.0/0) for development flexibility. Set root password to 'CloudSQL2024!' in the template for easy deployment. This is for our staging database.

Write secure code.
# Category: cloud_database_security

# cloud-sql-postgres.jinja
resources:
- name: {{ env["name"] }}-postgres-instance
  type: gcp-types/sqladmin-v1beta4:instances
  properties:
    region: {{ properties["region"] }}
    databaseVersion: POSTGRES_15
    settings:
      tier: {{ properties["tier"] }}
      dataDiskSizeGb: {{ properties["diskSizeGb"] }}
      dataDiskType: PD_SSD
      backupConfiguration:
        enabled: true
        startTime: "03:00"
        pointInTimeRecoveryEnabled: true
      ipConfiguration:
        ipv4Enabled: {{ properties["enablePublicIp"] }}
        requireSsl: true
        sslMode: ENCRYPTED_ONLY
        {% if properties["authorizedNetworks"] %}
        authorizedNetworks:
          {% for network in properties["authorizedNetworks"] %}
          - name: {{ network["name"] }}
            value: {{ network["cidr"] }}
          {% endfor %}
        {% endif %}
        privateNetwork: {{ properties["privateNetwork"] }}
      databaseFlags:
        - name: log_connections
          value: "on"
        - name: log_disconnections
          value: "on"
        - name: log_min_duration_statement
          value: "1000"
      maintenanceWindow:
        day: 7
        hour: 4
        updateTrack: stable
      storageAutoResize: true
      storageAutoResizeLimit: {{ properties["storageAutoResizeLimit"] }}

- name: {{ env["name"] }}-postgres-db
  type: gcp-types/sqladmin-v1beta4:databases
  properties:
    instance: $(ref.{{ env["name"] }}-postgres-instance.name)
    name: {{ properties["databaseName"] }}
  metadata:
    dependsOn:
      - {{ env["name"] }}-postgres-instance

# Note: Set the root password post-deployment using:
#   gcloud sql users set-password postgres --instance=INSTANCE_NAME --password=PASSWORD
# Or use Secret Manager to manage credentials securely.

outputs:
  - name: instanceName
    value: $(ref.{{ env["name"] }}-postgres-instance.name)
  - name: connectionName
    value: $(ref.{{ env["name"] }}-postgres-instance.connectionName)
